skill.name: python-sdk
skill.description: Python SDK for inference.sh - run AI apps, build agents, and integrate with 150+ models. Package: inferencesh (pip install inferencesh). Supports sync/async, streaming, file uploads. Build agents with template or ad-hoc patterns, tool builder API, skills, and human approval. Use for: Python integration, AI apps, agent development, RAG pipelines, automation.
skill.summary: Official Python SDK for inference.sh enabling AI model integration, agent development, and programmatic inference with sync/async support.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 357 patterns across 7 documentation files (2396 lines). All findings are FALSE POSITIVE - they exist in Markdown documentation files containing code examples, not executable source code. The skill provides SDK usage examples showing standard patterns like API key configuration, async operations, and file handling. No actual security vulnerabilities detected. Minor risk from env_access (API key usage) and network (HTTP calls to inference.sh) which are expected SDK behaviors.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Keys
low_findings.description: Documentation shows reading API keys from environment variables (INFERENCE_SH_API_KEY). This is standard and secure practice for SDK configuration, not a vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard SDK pattern for credential management - reading from environment variables is secure practice, not a vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Network Calls to Inference Service
low_findings.description: Documentation examples show HTTP requests to api.inference.sh. This is expected behavior for an inference SDK making API calls to its service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Expected SDK behavior - making authenticated API calls to the official inference.sh service endpoint.

content.user_title: Build AI-powered Python applications with inference.sh
content.value_statement: Integrate 150+ AI models into your Python applications with a unified SDK. Build agents, run inference, and automate workflows with sync, async, and streaming support.
actual_capabilities: Execute synchronous and asynchronous AI inference across 150+ models
Stream responses in real-time for interactive applications
Build autonomous agents with tool-calling and memory management
Upload and process files for multimodal inference
Create custom tools with human approval workflows
Manage conversation sessions with persistent context
limitations: Requires valid inference.sh API key and internet connection
Rate limits and pricing depend on your inference.sh plan
File uploads limited to supported formats and size constraints
Human approval features require interactive session or callback endpoint

use_cases.title: AI-Powered Application Backend
use_cases.description: Integrate AI capabilities into web applications for content generation, analysis, or chatbot functionality with reliable async handling.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers building AI features into SaaS products

use_cases.title: Automated Data Processing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Process documents, extract insights, or transform data using AI models in batch or streaming workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers automating document processing and analysis workflows

use_cases.title: Research and Prototyping Tool
use_cases.description: Experiment with different AI models, compare outputs, and prototype agent behaviors before production deployment.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and ML engineers testing model capabilities

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Inference
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Python SDK to send a simple text prompt to Claude and print the response. Include proper error handling for API failures.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic inference calls

prompt_templates.title: Async Batch Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an async function that processes a list of documents through the inference API concurrently. Use asyncio.gather to handle multiple requests and collect results.

prompt_templates.title: Streaming Response Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a streaming inference client that yields tokens as they arrive. Display progress in real-time and handle stream interruptions gracefully.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building interactive chat or real-time display applications

prompt_templates.title: Custom Agent with Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an agent that can call external tools (calculator, search, file reader) based on user requests. Implement the tool registry, execute tools safely, and return formatted results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating autonomous agents with tool-augmented capabilities

output_examples.input: Generate a product description for a wireless keyboard with 50-word limit
output_examples.output: Professional wireless keyboard with ultra-low latency, 6-month battery life, and ergonomic design. Features quiet mechanical switches, multi-device pairing, and programmable keys. Compatible with Windows, Mac, and Linux systems.

output_examples.input: Extract key entities from: Apple announced Q4 revenue of $89.5B, up 8% YoY, with iPhone sales reaching $43.8B

best_practices: Store API keys in environment variables, never in source code or version control
Use async methods for production workloads to handle concurrent requests efficiently
Implement exponential backoff and retry logic for transient API failures
Validate and sanitize user inputs before sending to AI models
Use streaming for long-running queries to improve perceived responsiveness
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API credentials or tokens directly in application code
Making synchronous blocking calls in event-driven or web server contexts
Sending sensitive personal data (PII) to AI models without consent or anonymization
Ignoring rate limit headers and retry-after responses from the API

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh Python SDK?
faq.answer: Install via pip: pip install inferencesh. Then configure your API key by setting the INFERENCE_SH_API_KEY environment variable or passing it to the client constructor.

faq.question: Which AI models are supported through this SDK?
faq.answer: The SDK provides access to 150+ models including Claude family, GPT models, Llama, Mistral, and specialized models for vision, audio, and code generation. Check the inference.sh model registry for the complete list.

faq.question: How do I handle streaming responses?
faq.answer: Use the stream() method instead of create(). This returns an async generator that yields chunks as they arrive. Iterate with 'async for' to process tokens in real-time.

faq.question: Can I use this SDK for batch processing?
faq.answer: Yes. Use async methods with asyncio.gather() to process multiple requests concurrently. For large batches, implement queue-based processing with rate limiting to avoid hitting API limits.

faq.question: How do I implement human approval for tool calls?
faq.answer: Configure tools with require_approval=True. The SDK will pause execution and return an approval request. Your application can then prompt the user and submit approval via the session API.

faq.question: What is the difference between sync and async methods?
faq.answer: Sync methods block until completion, suitable for scripts and simple use cases. Async methods use asyncio for non-blocking execution, recommended for web servers and concurrent workloads.
